expectations.text: The skill flags that one week is too early for a meaningful retro — the NEVER rules prohibit running a retro without outcome data
expectations.evidence: Opens with 'I can't run a meaningful retrospective on the search feature right now.' Explains: 'A product retrospective requires 4-12 weeks of outcome data.' Explicitly states: 'Running a retro without outcome data produces opinions and blame, not evidence-based learning.'

expectations.text: Recommends waiting 4-12 weeks for outcome data to accumulate — rushing a retro without data produces opinion-based blame, not evidence-based improvement
expectations.evidence: 'Schedule the retro for 6-8 weeks post-launch. By then you'll have enough data to evaluate whether the feature solved the problem.' Also includes a data collection checklist for the future retro.

expectations.text: May offer to run a delivery-focused Q2 review (on time? spec quality?) while deferring outcome questions
expectations.evidence: Offers: 'If you want to review how the delivery went while it's fresh, I can run a delivery-focused review covering Q2 (Did we build it as intended?)' with four specific questions that can be answered without outcome data.
